title: combretum tessmannii
intro: Combretum tessmannii, also known as the African bushwillow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Combretum tessmannii include the treatment of fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
